title: Sim-to-real project map
objective: Make the current twin, its evidence gaps, and the next admissible improvement legible to both researchers and bounded agents.

stages.title: Capture
stages.purpose: Create canonical, synchronized source episodes without silently admitting them to training or physical proof.

agent.boundary: Recorder commands remain loopback-only and operator-gated; saved episodes remain unadmitted source evidence.

stages.title: Scene
stages.purpose: Inspect visual reconstruction, scale, geometry, collision, and assumption boundaries before simulation claims.

agent.boundary: Visual reconstruction and scene proposals cannot grant metric, collision, evaluator, or physical authority.

stages.title: Simulate
stages.purpose: Run and inspect the canonical MuJoCo embodiment while keeping visual context and physics authority separate.

agent.boundary: Candidate generation does not promote itself; action-frozen comparisons preserve exact source action bytes.

stages.title: Replay
stages.purpose: Compare recorded reality, the same-camera visual twin, and receipt-bound action-frozen physics on one timeline.

agent.boundary: Missing physics remains missing; visual similarity never substitutes for a simulator or task consequence.

stages.title: Evaluate
stages.purpose: Apply deterministic gates and dense diagnostic margins without allowing a model or dashboard to score itself.

agent.boundary: The evaluator owns thresholds, held-outs, consequence gates, and promotion decisions.

stages.title: Diagnose
stages.purpose: Trace the first divergence through residuals, mechanisms, hypotheses, interventions, posterior movement, and consequence.

agent.boundary: Agents receive compact evidence reports and may propose hypotheses; missing fields stay missing and advice is not evaluator evidence.

stages.title: Improve
stages.purpose: Choose one bounded intervention, correction, or data request through the governed outer loop.

agent.boundary: The Learning Factory and task orchestrator constrain execution; agents do not own budgets, sealed evaluation, admission, or promotion.

stages.title: Learn + transfer
stages.purpose: Publish admitted datasets, train bounded candidates, evaluate held-outs, and open physical canaries only through separate authority.

agent.boundary: No Studio endpoint grants training, promotion, gateway, camera, serial, torque, robot-motion, or physical-task authority.
